mnemonics: Third Edition is like a blockchain's third draft: Everyone (the network) must agree (consensus) on the final, permanent (immutable) version, just like a published book.
keyPoints: The 'Third Edition' refers to a significant, non-backwards-compatible upgrade or hard fork of a blockchain protocol.
Such an upgrade requires consensus from the network participants to adopt the new set of rules.
It results in a permanent split from the previous version, creating a new transaction history from the point of the fork.
commonMistakes: Confusing a hard fork (Third Edition) with a soft fork, which is backwards-compatible.
Assuming the old chain always disappears; both the new and old chains can continue to exist separately (e.g., Ethereum and Ethereum Classic).
Believing that all nodes are automatically forced to upgrade; participation is voluntary and requires consent to the new rules.
